meaning: adj.腼腆的,怕羞的,卖弄风情的
examples: She gave me a coy smile.
She was a little coy about how much her dress cost.
Mr. Alexander is not the slightest bit coy about his ambitions.
Ross has been coy about whether he will step in and invest in Ambac or other bond insurers.
Mrs Clinton has been coy about a possible White House run in four years' time.
